metric_value: Focusing on core competencies: IT excellence best achieved by outsider allowing management more time for non-IT decisions
notes: First of five major reasons enterprises choose to outsource IT

metric_value: Saving money is primary expectation though palming off dysfunctional IT on naive outsourcer is a pipe dream
notes: Second driver; note caveat about unrealistic expectations

metric_value: IT resource constraints: lack of talent, costly talent, staff turnover, wrong IT skill-sets in rapidly changing company
notes: Third driver; multifaceted talent challenge

metric_value: Time-to-market concern in competitive industries where IT agility is critical; outsourcer size skills and agility often cited
notes: Fourth driver; particularly relevant in fast-moving industries

metric_value: Culture wars: line-of-business management fed up with IT intransigence demands change including nuclear blow-it-up outsourcing option
notes: Fifth driver; most dramatic option reflecting organizational dysfunction

metric_value: Approximately 30% of IT outsourcing engagements are seriously misaligned with intended scope and contracted terms
notes: Hypothesis based on Aberdeen research; misalignment from poor scope understanding or inaccurate internal metrics

metric_value: 50-66% of IT outsourcing contracts running at sub-optimal levels as measured by customer perception and internal performance metrics
notes: Hypothesis for mainstream outsourcing tier; largest population segment

metric_value: Only 5-10% of multi-million dollar IT outsourcing agreements driving all anticipated business value to the buyer
notes: Smallest tier representing true outsourcing partnership success

metric_value: Cannot name a single company that has outsourced IT functions and not learned some painful lessons not to be repeated
notes: Strong assertion suggesting universal learning curve in IT outsourcing

metric_value: Service Level Agreements: success in setting maintaining measuring and aligning SLAs with business units
notes: First of nine challenge areas for sub-optimal outsourcing

metric_value: Governance: process executive responsible capacity management process and ability to execute before and after contract signing
notes: Second challenge area; spans entire outsourcing lifecycle

metric_value: Preparation: HR project management process and infrastructure fixes and freezes and outsourcer support levels during prep stage
notes: Third challenge area; pre-transition readiness

metric_value: Transition: process and success in transitioning staff infrastructure business process and users to new outsourced mode
notes: Fourth challenge area; critical handoff phase

metric_value: Operations: operational management issues and challenges running routinely after transition
notes: Fifth challenge area; steady-state management

metric_value: Change Management: how planned and unplanned changes map to the model anticipated in the outsourcing contract
notes: Sixth challenge area; contract flexibility and adaptation

metric_value: Pricing: how planned and unplanned pricing in contract maps to actual results and reasons for discrepancies
notes: Seventh challenge area; cost governance

metric_value: Conflict Resolution: pervasiveness depth and ongoing nature of conflicts; contractual steps vs stepping outside contract; remediation vs litigation
notes: Eighth challenge area; relationship management

metric_value: Metrics: how companies performed before and after outsourcing and how that maps to IT and management maturity
notes: Ninth challenge area; measurement and accountability

metric_value: Over 50% of best-in-class companies measure ROI after every software application upgrade or change
notes: From Figure 1; IT maturity indicator used as proxy for outsourcing readiness

metric_value: Over one-third of average companies measure ROI after every change
notes: From Figure 1; middle tier of IT maturity

metric_value: Over 50% of laggard IT organizations do not measure ROI after changes
notes: From Figure 1; lowest tier of IT maturity

metric_value: Over 2000 companies surveyed on IT organizational maturity in past two years
notes: Aberdeen's extensive survey base providing empirical foundation for study hypotheses

metric_value: Range from simple application outsourcing (e.g. email) to full business process offshoring of major processes (e.g. general ledger or accounts payable)
notes: Spectrum of outsourcing options available to companies of all sizes

metric_value: Full business process offshoring of major processes such as general ledger or accounts payable represents high end of outsourcing spectrum
notes: BPO positioned as most extensive outsourcing option

metric_value: Similar maturity spectrum that affects IT organizational performance also effects outsourcing outcomes
notes: Core hypothesis linking IT maturity model to outsourcing success prediction

metric_value: Planned benchmark report combining survey and interview techniques across Global 5000 companies across all industries
notes: Methodology for planned full benchmark study announced for late June 2006

metric_value: Aberdeen began quantitative tracking of IT planning issues in 2004
notes: Establishes longitudinal data collection timeline for outsourcing observations

metric_value: SOA in IT Benchmark Report published December 2005 as related research
notes: Cross-reference to Aberdeen SOA benchmark; SOA relevant to outsourcing architecture

metric_value: Achieving More Value from Enterprise Integration Benchmark Report published May 2006
notes: Cross-reference providing IT maturity data used in Figure 1

metric_value: Aberdeen Group acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2007 approximately one year after publishing this research brief
notes: Post-study outcome; Aberdeen continued as research brand under new ownership

metric_value: Aberdeen Group ultimately acquired by Spiceworks Ziff Davis; brand continued but original firm no longer independent
notes: Long-term outcome for the analyst firm that authored this study

metric_value: High level of dissatisfaction with outsourcing in general indicated by prior Aberdeen research
notes: Foundation premise driving the planned benchmark study

metric_value: Industry surveys consistently show 40-60% dissatisfaction rates with IT outsourcing arrangements through 2010s and 2020s
notes: Validates Aberdeen's hypothesis that most outsourcing runs sub-optimally

metric_value: Outsourcing governance framework including conflict resolution and change management identified as critical success differentiator
notes: Predicted that governance capability would separate optimal from sub-optimal outsourcing

metric_value: Outsourcing governance frameworks became standard practice; advisory firms (Gartner ISG Everest) built entire practices around outsourcing governance maturity models
notes: Confirms Aberdeen's emphasis on governance as differentiator; the maturity model approach persists
